mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations and compress corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or trigger fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production during a confirmed economic expansion reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth during expansion regimes yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.0, persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate a healthy labor market that supports wage growth and consumer expenditure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are negative in > 50% of positive payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high real GDP growth and elevated inflation indicate an overheating economy that triggers restrictive monetary policy and supply chain bottlenecks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high growth and high inflation fails to predict negative 20d returns with statistical significance, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining federal funds rates reflects accommodative monetary policy supporting manufacturing output, which boosts corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM change > 0 and FEDFUNDS MoM change < 0
failure_condition: The combination of rising production and falling rates yields negative 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.
